Electric Vehicle Webinar 2024 June 28, 2024 12:00PM - 1:00PM Electric Car Charging Station Electric Car Charging Station 1/1 Fully Remote (Zoom) Event Details Electric vehicles have an electric motor instead of a traditional internal combustion engine. The motor runs by drawing electricity from a battery that can be charged from an external source. Additionally, electric vehicles often feature braking systems that capture and use lost energy to recharge the battery. Switching to electric vehicles is one of the best strategies to stop global warming. As of 2022, fossil fuel vehicles account for about 10% of the world's greenhouse gas emissions. But if these vehicles become electric, we could cut almost 20% of global emissions. Furthermore, EV vehicles have numerous advantages, such as cleaner air and quieter cities. On June 28th, 2024, HNP Canada co-hosted an online webinar

Sponsorships Partnerships DONATE More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. Centennial Park Cleanup In Partnership With Our Sponsors! April 2023 11:00AM - 2:00PM image DSCN3582 IMG_9533 image 1/13 Special Thank You to Our Sponsors! ChocoSol Wholesale Event Details Thank you to everyone who participated in our Centennial Park Clean-Up! This event provided a wonderful opportunity for like-minded individuals to connect, make a tangible positive impact on the community, and gain valuable volunteer hours. Centennial Park, spanning 526 acres, is one of Toronto's largest public parks, attracting approximately 1.5 million visitors annually. Volunteer cleanups are crucial as they help protect the environment and provide clean spaces that benefit both people and aquatic life, particularly given the park's popularity for fishing. The need for such cleanups is underscored by severe environmental concerns. Littering alone costs the city up to $250,000 annually.

description: 2:00 pm - 4:00 pm Gardening for Wildlife Workshop April 26 @ 2:00 pm - 4:00 pm Gardening for Wildlife Workshop Join us for an engaging 'Gardening for Wildlife' workshop with our friends at South Coast Gardens, hosted by the Hamilton Naturalists' Club & the Bruleville Nature Park Urban Forest Stewardship Project! Learn how to design a space that truly supports birds, pollinators, and more - using native plants, layering for food and shelter, and creating […]

title: Iroquoia BTC Naturalist Hike Series- Grimsby Wetlands
location: Hamilton Naturalists' Club, Bruce McKenzie, for a tour of the award-winning Grimsby Wetl
description: 6:30 pm - 9:00 pm Iroquoia BTC Naturalist Hike Series- Grimsby Wetlands April 30 @ 6:30 pm - 9:00 pm Iroquoia BTC Naturalist Hike Series- Grimsby Wetlands Please join BTC hike leader, Ruth Moffatt, and well-known Hamilton Naturalists' Club, Bruce McKenzie, for a tour of the award-winning Grimsby Wetlands. Bruce will describe the history of this 25 year old wetland from its beginnings as a wastewater treatment area to its notoriety for birding, now. A cinnamon teal duck was spotted here a […]

title: Garlic Mustard Pull
description: 10:00 am - 1:00 pm Garlic Mustard Pull May 2 @ 10:00 am - 1:00 pm Garlic Mustard Pull Join us for a morning of removing invasive Garlic Mustard at our Spooky Hollow Nature Sanctuary and enjoy the beautiful views of mature Carolinian forest. Depending on the time we may take a hike around one of the trail loops as well. Everyone is welcome and carpooling is encouraged. Gloves will be provided. Pulling will […]

description: Browse our programs Browse our programs We have a variety of options to help you on your mental health journey. In some cases, intake is required. Simply put, intake is a session by appointment with a Stella’s Place Intake Social Worker to gather some information, understand your goals, and determine a support plan. At Stella’s Place, we provide free mental health services for young adults aged 16 to 29 and living in Toronto (with an M postal code). Our programs are divided into 3 categories: drop-in , by appointment and registered . For appointments and registered programs, you may be added to a waitlist. BeanBagChat Get one-on-one support online. Click the chat icon in the lower right corner on any page on www.stellasplace.ca to start chatting with a counsellor. How to Get Connected Individual Intake Discuss what programs you are interested in with Access Coordinators and get placed on a waitlist or signed up for a drop-in program. description: Maple Youth Wellness Hub Welcome to the Maple Youth Wellness Hub! We are a safe, welcoming space offering Integrated Youth Services (IYS) for young people all across York Region aged 12 to 25 and their families looking for mental health care, support with substance use concerns, and more. These services are free of charge, available on walk-in or appointment, and no health card is required. The Hub is located at 9401 Jane Street, Unit 321, Third Floor Maple, On. We are located right across Maple’s Auto mall and Canada’s Wonderland. In Crisis? Click Here Get to know the Maple hub Community-Based & Accessible: Located in Vaughan, the hub serves a diverse youth population with easy access to services. location: Scarborough resident, community activist, Director & Co-founder of Scarborough Environmenta
description: 2025 Green Hearts Awardee – Krissan Veerasingam Apr 2, 2025 "This year GNN is thrilled to celebrate Krissan Veerasingam, a lifelong Scarborough resident, community activist, Director & Co-founder of Scarborough Environmental Association (SEA), and Board Member of TTC riders. ... GNN’s first contact with Krissan was in the “tell us more” box when he signed up to join in 2022. He said he and 5 others wanted to start a new Scarborough based group to push Scarborough councillors to prioritize equitable environmental policy. And so, his journey as the co-founder of SEA began."

location: Toronto Podcast: A Mover and Shaker in Scarborough Jun 30, 2024 You need a good pair of
description: Hidden Gems Toronto Podcast: A Mover and Shaker in Scarborough Jun 30, 2024 You need a good pair of running shoes to keep up with Krissan Veerasingam. He helped launch the Scarborough Repair Café where residents can bring a multitude of household items for repair, free of charge. He is also an advocate for public transit and supports numerous environmental initiatives. As if this wasn't enough, he finds time to promote ethnic grocery stores and restaurants throughout Scarborough. And he does all this will great enthusiasm and aplomb. Residents are fortunate to have him in their corner.

location: Scarborough residents has become an unofficial tourism board Feb 3, 2024 "If you think of w
description: How a group of young, food-obsessed Scarborough residents has become an unofficial tourism board Feb 3, 2024 "If you think of what ties a neighbourhood together, it's places where people go," says Veerasingam, who led a food tour last summer highlighting places to eat along the now-shuttered Scarborough RT line. "You’ll see familiar faces week after week in places where you build connections with other customers and the people who work there."

title: Stuck on the bus: Toronto's 2024 budget offers no relief for Scarborough’s weary commuters Jan 13, 2024 Residents and transit advocates see the lack of busway funding as the latest
location: Toronto's 2024 budget offers no relief for Scarborough’s weary commuters Jan 13, 2024 R
description: Stuck on the bus: Toronto's 2024 budget offers no relief for Scarborough’s weary commuters Jan 13, 2024 Residents and transit advocates see the lack of busway funding as the latest example of how the area has been neglected by politicians at city hall and Queen’s Park. Successive administrations have spent decades playing political ping-pong with the replacement for the aging RT — at one point it was going to be an LRT — ultimately leaving Scarborough residents today without rapid transit for years. “Sometimes it feels like the city views Scarborough residents’ time as less valuable," said Krissan Veerasingam, board member for transit advocacy group TTCriders.

location: Toronto organized Repair Café this past year. In particular, Scarborough Environment As
description: Repair Café takes big strides with our new leaders! Dec 31, 2023 A good number of community organizations in Toronto organized Repair Café this past year. In particular, Scarborough Environment Association (SEA) began to offer Repair Café monthly. Krissan and Fizza, at SEA, share about their new Repair Café startup and what Repair Café means to them.

location: Toronto’s transportation system.
description: Fizza Khalid Speaks at Moving Together Conference Nov 14, 2023 Environmental Defence hosted Moving Together: Creating the Transportation Future We Want, an event moderated by Uytae Lee of CBC’s Stories About Here. The gathering brought together community experts like Fizza Khalid and engaged the public in discussions on building a clean and equitable transportation future. Mayor Olivia Chow attended and shared her vision for the future of Toronto’s transportation system.

description: TTC looking at options as Scarborough RT remains out of service following derailment Aug 16, 2023 "I think they're making the best of a bad situation, but they could make better of a bad situation and that's why I advocate for the rapid lanes, and of course the busway," said Krissan Veerasingam with TTC Riders. "We need to see these solutions like bus lanes and, more importantly, a busway because, of course, you can't fit 70 buses an hour realistically on a Scarborough street."

title: Tenants protest province's Bill 23 Nov 23, 2022 Fizza Khalid of the Scarborough Environmental Association said the only way to help the environment is through increasing housing de
location: Scarborough Environmental Association said the only way to help the environment is through
description: Tenants protest province's Bill 23 Nov 23, 2022 Fizza Khalid of the Scarborough Environmental Association said the only way to help the environment is through increasing housing density in cities, but she said she believes the Progressive Conservative government and developers want to build more suburban sprawl in Ontario’s Greenbelt instead. “I have a full-time job; I went to university. I can’t even afford a place to live,” Khalid said. “You expect me to move out to the Greenbelt.”

description: Vaughan International Music Festival August 23 – 24 + August 23 – 24 Vaughan International Music Festival The VIMF, Established As The 1st International Music Festival In The City Of Vaughan And York Region, Celebrates Diversity, Culture And Inclusiveness Through Music. The Festival Showcases A Wide Range Of Musical Genres, Including Pop, Hip-Hop, Rock, Classical, Jazz And World Music, And Features Performances By Both Established And Emerging Artists. title: Vaughan International Film Festival
location: Vaughan International Film Festival June 17 – 19 + June 17 – 19 Vaughan International F
description: Vaughan International Film Festival June 17 – 19 + June 17 – 19 Vaughan International Film Festival The 11th Vaughan International Film Festival (VFF) launches its 2024 edition with the Student Screening at Assembly Park on June 17, showcasing 18 short films created by high school students from across Ontario. During the event, three finalists will be announced and awarded Lumix camera equipment, followed by a networking session with industry professionals. As a not-for-profit organization near Toronto, VFF is dedicated to advancing the short film industry through screenings, scholarships, and educational events. The festival serves as a vital platform for emerging and established filmmakers, drawing an audience of over 20,000 attendees each year. The full lineup can be found at vaughanfilmfestival.com and tickets can be purchased here .

The hugely popular food truck event series, which ran weekly from 2015 to 2022 in the GTA is back this summer at Assembly Park. The expansive public space, located close to countless retail and transit connections, will feature a daily assembly of Toronto’s best food trucks from June 7 to Sept. 27, 2024. As part of this summer series, Food Truck’N Friday will be bringing its unique concept that combines food truck eats, artisans and entertainment to the park on every Friday. “Food Truck’N Friday at Assembly Park is tapped to become a hot new summer destination for the Greater Toronto Area,” says Jason Bellissimo, Founder of Food Truck’N Friday and Principal at J.B. title: Mashup Pon di Road
description: Mashup Pon di Road July 19 + July 19 Mashup Pon di Road MASHUP PON DI ROAD is a bashment circus on a big truck—an outdoor, vaudeville-style, West-Indian flavoured, black girl-powered variety show pulling up on city streets. Fusing raucous humour with whip-smart cultural critique, MASHUP PON DI ROAD pokes fun at the constraints of the patriarchy, the expectations of womanhood, and the end of the world as we know it. through a series of vignettes and songs, two ringmasters and their band of colourful misfits rum punch up at the ruling class, giving you a funhouse mirror look at the sheer absurdity of dis oppressive world. the path to liberation sparkles with laughter. let’s get unruly. Touching road summer 2022. Tickets are free – register your spot with the link below – come one, come all! Welcome to di circus! Community Preview: July 19, 2022 , gates at 8 p.m. / show at 9 p.m. @ Assembly Park – 80 Interchange Way – Sponsored by QuadReal Property Group REGISTER YOUR SPOT HERE
